core.description: orchestrator/gates/state-machine/spool dispatch -- rebuilds on any plugkit-core src change

embed-weights.description: BERT embedding model weights, served natively by gm-runner's host_vec_embed; also embedded in the browser/wasm-fallback build variant

note: RESOLVED (treesitter-grammar-runtime-split): plugkit-core no longer statically links any tree-sitter grammar or the tree-sitter core crate. crates/plugkit-core/Cargo.toml carries zero tree-sitter-* dependencies as of this note; code_index.rs::extract_chunks parses exclusively via call_plugin("treesitter", "parse", {lang, source}) over the host_plugin_call wasm-import ABI (see wasm_dispatch/host_abi.rs), the same generic out-of-process-plugin mechanism already proven for libsql/bert. The wasm host (agentplug-runner, both the JS-wrapper loader and the native gm-runner loader) resolves/downloads a standalone treesitter.wasm plugin at ~/.agentplug/plugins/treesitter.wasm and dispatches `parse` for all 18 languages code_index.rs's lang_for_ext maps to (verified live: javascript/typescript/python/rust/go/c/cpp/java/json/html/css/bash/markdown/csharp/haskell/julia/php/powershell/ruby all return {ok:true,nodes:[...]} via `agentplug-runner dispatch treesitter parse`). The entries below now document grammar CONTENT version pins for that external treesitter plugin build (not plugkit-core's own Cargo.toml, which no longer resolves them) -- kept here as the still-useful cross-repo version-pin ledger versus rs-codeinsight's independent 0.24.7-pinned in-process grammar set, updated to reflect the plugin now being the sole consumer on the rs-plugkit side.

tree_sitter_cross_repo_manifest.purpose: Single source of truth for tree-sitter GRAMMAR version pins shared between rs-plugkit (crates/plugkit-core, AST-node-boundary extraction for embedding chunks, code_index.rs) and rs-codeinsight (per-language metrics extraction for conventions/depgraph output, analyzer.rs). These are two genuinely independent crates with different extraction purposes -- NOT a shared dependency, NOT a candidate for crate merge -- this section only pins the grammar version each repo resolves to when both use the same language grammar, so the two repos never silently drift on which exact grammar release they parse with.

core_pin_intentionally_diverges.reason: The tree-sitter CORE crate (the parser runtime API, not a language grammar) is pinned independently per repo by design -- rs-plugkit bumped to 0.26 this session for its own reasons; rs-codeinsight's broader 28-language optional-feature grammar set has not been re-validated against 0.26 yet. This is the genuine API-incompatibility surface named in treesitter-surface-consolidation-version-drift-documented and is NOT covered by this manifest -- only per-language GRAMMAR crate versions are pinned here, never the tree-sitter core version itself.

shared_grammars.description: Every language grammar both repos currently depend on, and its pinned version. Owner = which repo's Cargo.toml is authoritative when a version bump is needed; the other repo's Cargo.toml must be bumped in lockstep in the same PR/session. All entries below were verified to already resolve identically in both Cargo.lock files as of this manifest's authoring.

codeinsight_only_grammars.description: Languages rs-codeinsight supports via its broader 28-language optional-feature set that rs-plugkit does not integrate at all -- these have no shared-pin obligation, owned solely by rs-codeinsight, listed here only for completeness so a future grammar addition to rs-plugkit checks this list first instead of independently picking a version.

tree_sitter_cross_repo_manifest.maintenance_rule: When bumping any shared grammar's version in either repo's Cargo.toml, update this manifest in the SAME commit and bump the other repo's Cargo.toml to match in the same session -- this file is the check, not a passive record. A grammar bump landing in one repo without the paired bump + manifest update here is itself a version-drift regression of the exact kind this row exists to close.
